features.name: Model Validation
features.description: Type-safe data validation using Python type annotations with automatic coercion and error reporting.

features.name: Serialization
features.description: Model serialization to dict, JSON, and custom formats with include/exclude field control.

features.name: Custom Validators
features.description: Field and model-level validators using @field_validator and @model_validator decorators.

features.name: Computed Fields
features.description: Dynamically computed properties that appear in serialized output via the @computed_field decorator.

features.name: Generic Models
features.description: Support for generic type parameters in BaseModel subclasses using Python's Generic type system.

features.name: JSON Schema Generation
features.description: Automatic JSON Schema generation from model definitions for API documentation and validation.

features.name: Settings Management
features.description: Environment variable and config file loading into typed settings models via BaseSettings.

call_chain.role: Entry point for model instantiation — delegates to the __pydantic_validator__ for data validation.
Metaclass that processes field definitions, builds the core schema, and compiles the validator at class creation time.
Walks the model's field annotations and builds a pydantic-core compatible validation schema.
Collects and resolves all field definitions from annotations, Field() calls, and inherited fields.
Factory function for creating FieldInfo objects with constraints, defaults, and metadata.

model_validation.explanation: Pydantic validation happens in two phases. At class definition time, ModelMetaclass.__new__() collects all annotated fields via collect_model_fields(), then GenerateSchema builds a core validation schema that maps each field to its type and constraints. This schema is compiled into a high-performance Rust-based validator via pydantic-core. At runtime, BaseModel.__init__() passes input data to this compiled validator, which performs type coercion, constraint checking, and error collection in a single pass. Failed validations raise ValidationError with detailed error locations.

call_chain.role: Walks the core schema and builds a JSON Schema dict with proper $ref handling for nested models.
Builds the JSON Schema for a model type including properties, required fields, and additional properties.
Manages the $defs section, creating references for reusable model schemas to avoid duplication.
Class method that instantiates the schema generator and produces the complete JSON Schema output.

json_schema.explanation: JSON Schema generation starts from BaseModel.model_json_schema() which creates a GenerateJsonSchema instance. The generate() method walks the Pydantic core schema tree, converting each node type (str, int, model, union, list, etc.) into its JSON Schema equivalent. Models are handled by model_schema() which maps fields to properties. Nested models are deduplicated using $ref references managed by get_defs_ref(), creating a $defs section at the schema root. The output conforms to JSON Schema Draft 2020-12.
